title: Senator Larry Boggs Supported by Pro-Abortion Democrat Sen. Mary Boren
author: Sam Riley
orientation: Free the States argues that incumbent Oklahoma Senator Larry Boggs is exposed as functionally unserious about abortion because pro-abortion Democrat Senator Mary Boren publicly defended and endorsed him over abolitionist challenger Warren Hamilton in the SD7 race. The article uses Boren's pro-Roe rhetoric to indict Boggs' record of backing toothless pro-life bills.
audiences: Oklahoma SD7 voter weighing Boggs vs. Hamilton
Pro-life Republican skeptical of abolitionist primary challenges
Abolitionist activist tracking incremental vs. immediate abolition
Pastor or church leader evaluating candidate endorsements
Political journalist covering the Haskell County GOP confrontation

questions.question: What incident triggered the Facebook debate that exposed Sen. Mary Boren's support for Larry Boggs?
questions.audience: Political journalist covering the Haskell County GOP confrontation
questions.answer: A debate erupted on JJ Francais' Facebook post in the wake of the July 14 Haskell County GOP meeting at which Boggs physically shoved his primary challenger Warren Hamilton and threatened one of Hamilton's supporters.
questions.quote: Shortly after the July 14th Haskell County GOP fiasco where Larry Boggs shoved his opponent Warren Hamilton and threatened one of Hamilton's supporters, a debate broke out on a Facebook post by local writer JJ Francais.

questions.question: How does Sen. Mary Boren describe Roe v. Wade on her own website?
questions.audience: Pro-life Republican skeptical of abolitionist primary challenges
questions.answer: Boren frames Roe as well-settled precedent that supposedly balances the interests of the woman, her unborn child, and the state, treating it as a protection of women's privacy, dignity, and moral agency.
questions.quote: Roe v. Wade effectively balances the interests of the woman*, her un-born [sic] child*, and the state.  Roe v. Wade is accepted as well settled [sic] precedent and protects the privacy, dignity, and moral agency of women.

questions.question: Why does the author call Boren's reasoning sociopathic rather than merely mistaken?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist tracking incremental vs. immediate abolition
questions.answer: Because Boren explicitly acknowledges the unborn as a child and yet endorses procedures that decapitate, suffocate, and dismember that child, the author argues her position is not ignorance but a deliberate endorsement of murder.
questions.quote: The fact that she recognizes them as a child and endorses wholesale murder is astonishing. Though it is typical of abortion supporters, this position and reasoning is nothing short of sociopathic.

questions.question: What does the article claim Boren's endorsement reveals about Larry Boggs' legislative record?
questions.audience: Oklahoma SD7 voter weighing Boggs vs. Hamilton
questions.answer: It claims Boren naturally prefers Boggs because he sponsors pro-life bills that accomplish nothing, leaving abortion legal, instead of pushing for the immediate and total abolition of abortion that Warren Hamilton would.
questions.quote: Unsurprisingly, she prefers Boggs who supports worthless pro-life bills that do nothing, as opposed to a candidate who is going to consistently push for immediate and total abolition.

questions.question: How did the Boggs campaign respond to receiving a pro-abortion senator's public support?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader evaluating candidate endorsements
questions.answer: Rather than disavowing it, Boggs' campaign manager Connor Alford liked Boren's comments, signaling the campaign welcomed the endorsement of an avowed pro-abortion radical.
questions.quote: Her comments in support of Boggs were even liked by Boggs' campaign manager Connor Alford. They happily took the support of the pro-abortion radical.

questions.question: How does the author respond to Boren and the local journalist labeling abolitionists a "cult"?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist tracking incremental vs. immediate abolition
questions.answer: He calls the label hypocritical, noting Free the States merely told the truth about Smalley keeping abortion legal while it is the pro-abortion side that resorts to slanderous name-calling and victim posturing.
questions.quote: Name-calling is their game, not ours; hence "cult." This is the game they play, disparaging abolitionists with all sorts of slanderous names and accusations while playing the victim because we call them out on substantive grounds for keeping abortion legal.

questions.question: What concrete voting recommendation does the article give SD7 voters?
questions.audience: Oklahoma SD7 voter weighing Boggs vs. Hamilton
questions.answer: It urges voters who care about preborn children and consistent conservatism to reject the candidate endorsed by a pro-Roe senator and instead cast their ballot for Warren Hamilton.
questions.quote: Don't choose the guy supported by a Senator who believes that decapitating, poisoning, and ripping preborn children to pieces effectively "balances their interests." Vote for Warren Hamilton.

related.title: Responding to Larry Boggs' Explanation of Tuesday's Confrontation
related.reason: The article links directly to this piece as the source for debunking Boggs' account of the Haskell County shoving incident.

related.title: James Lankford Should Revoke His Endorsement of Larry Boggs
related.reason: Another Free the States piece arguing Boggs is unworthy of pro-life endorsements, parallel to the Boren critique.

related.title: Larry Boggs Campaigns With 2019 & 2020 Abortion Rights Men of the Year
related.reason: Reinforces the thesis that Boggs aligns with figures hostile to abolition, matching the Boren-endorsement narrative.

related.title: Disgraceful! Pro-Life Establishment Lies About Abolitionist Candidate Warren Hamilton
related.reason: Defends Hamilton, the abolitionist challenger this article tells voters to support against Boggs.

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists for Abolishing Abortion Endorses Warren Hamilton for SD7
related.reason: A counterweight endorsement for Hamilton that contrasts with Boren's endorsement of Boggs.

related.title: Warren Hamilton Defeats Larry Boggs, Margie Alfonso Defeats Clay Iiams
related.reason: Reports the SD7 election outcome that this article was urging voters toward.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Provides the broader argument behind the article's claim that Boggs' pro-life bills are 'worthless' and do nothing.
